title: proustia pungens var. cuneifolia
intro: Proustia pungens var. cuneifolia, also known as 'Huinasto' in Quechua, is a plant species in the daisy family, native to the Andean highlands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Proustia pungens var. cuneifolia include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. Traditionally, this plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction, often mixed with other medicinal plants, and is consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment.
